en.extract:
The black cuckooshrike (Campephaga flava) is a species of bird in the cuckooshrike family Campephagidae. The species is closely related to Petit's cuckooshrike and the red-shouldered cuckooshrike, and forms a superspecies with them. It is also known as the African black cuckooshrike.
It is found in Angola, Botswana, Burundi, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Namibia, Rwanda, Somalia, South Africa, South Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, dry savanna, and subtropical or tropical dry shrubland.


References


External links

Black Cuckooshrike - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.

The '''black cuckooshrike''' ('''''Campephaga flava''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the cuckooshrike family [[Campephagidae]]. The species is closely related to [[Petit's cuckooshrike]] and the [[red-shouldered cuckooshrike]], and forms a [[Species complex|superspecies]] with them. It is also known as the '''African black cuckooshrike'''.<ref name="HBW" >Taylor, B. (2017). Black Cuckoo-shrike (''Campephaga flava''). In: del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A., Sargatal, J., Christie, D.A. & de Juana, E. (eds.). Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona. (retrieved from http://www.hbw.com/node/57897 on 10 March 2017).</ref>

It is found in [[Angola]], [[Botswana]], [[Burundi]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Eswatini]], [[Ethiopia]], [[Kenya]], [[Malawi]], [[Mozambique]], [[Namibia]], [[Rwanda]], [[Somalia]], [[South Africa]], [[South Sudan]], [[Tanzania]], [[Uganda]], [[Zambia]], and [[Zimbabwe]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s, dry [[savanna]], and subtropical or tropical dry [[shrubland]].
